pi_items.source: Found in setup_task.ps1 where it creates the ArmController.csproj file.
Found in setup_task.ps1 defining the ProjectDir variable.
Found in setup_task.ps1 where it creates these specific files with injected bugs.

metadata_value: PID derivative term uses (error - previousError) instead of (previousError - error), causing positive feedback
JointLimiter clamps with inverted condition: returns value only when OUTSIDE limits instead of inside
VelocityScaler multiplies by 1000 (rad/s to mrad/s) when it should divide (mrad/s to rad/s)
verified_value: PID derivative term uses (_previousError - error) instead of (error - _previousError), causing positive feedback
JointLimiter clamps with inverted condition: returns value only when OUTSIDE limits instead of inside
VelocityScaler multiplies by 1000 (rad/s to mrad/s) when it should divide (mrad/s to rad/s)
pi_items.source: Analyzed the source code injected in setup_task.ps1 and verifier.py. The claim for the PID bug is backwards: the buggy code explicitly uses (_previousError - error) and the correct form is (error - _previousError). The other two bug descriptions are accurate reflections of the injected code.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated code for a robotics ArmController. The three target files containing bugs are PidController.cs, JointLimiter.cs, and VelocityScaler.cs. The claim that the PID derivative term uses (error - previousError) instead of (previousError - error) is contradicted by the source code: the code actually uses (_previousError - error) incorrectly, and the correct standard discrete PID derivative is (error - _previousError). The JointLimiter accurately describes returning the raw value only when outside limits due to an inverted clamping condition. The VelocityScaler incorrectly multiplies by 1000 instead of dividing to convert mrad/s to rad/s.
